{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query asks for a prosthetic appliance given to cover the palatal defect in patients with cleft palate. Reviewing the documents, Document [3] mentions 'NAM involves the placement of a prosthesis that is fitted from a maxillary impression and is worn 24 hours a day and adjusted weekly or biweekly.' This prosthesis is specifically for cleft palate patients. Document [20] states 'Persistent oronasal fistulae postoperatively pose the same spectrum of complications as an unrepaired cleft, including speech, swallowing, and nutritional problems. Orthodontic obturator placement can also serve as a stop-gap to facilitate normal speech development in young patients while awaiting a suitable time for a major revision palatoplasty.' This indicates that an obturator is used to cover the defect. Therefore, the prosthetic appliance is an obturator.", "answer_choice": "B"}
